title: gypsophila polygonoides subsp. ocellata
intro: Gypsophila polygonoides subsp. ocellata, also known as Ocellated Gypsophila, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Gypsophila polygonoides subsp. ocellata include its potential to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate coughs. Herbal preparations of Gypsophila polygonoides subsp. ocellata can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in folk medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health complaints.
